title: YouTube upload and thumbnail
analysis:
This task creates two new modules under autopilot/upload/ and one helper script.

youtube.py: Implements upload_video() using google-api-python-client (already in pyproject.toml dependencies). Needs OAuth 2.0 credential loading from YouTubeConfig.credentials_path, token refresh for unattended operation, YouTube Data API v3 upload with metadata from the narrative/script/activity-cluster data in CatalogDB, and storage of the YouTube URL/ID in the existing uploads table via db.insert_upload().

thumbnail.py: Implements extract_best_thumbnail() that scores candidate frames from a video using three criteria: YOLO detection confidence (from detections table), rule-of-thirds composition score, and sharpness (Laplacian variance via cv2/numpy). Extracts the best frame as JPEG and uploads it via YouTube thumbnails.set API.

scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py: One-time interactive script that runs the OAuth 2.0 installed-app flow, opens a browser for consent, and saves the refresh token to credentials_path.

Existing infrastructure to reuse:
- CatalogDB.insert_upload() / get_upload() already exists with the uploads table schema
- YouTubeConfig dataclass with privacy_status, default_category, credentials_path
- Narrative/script/activity-cluster data already in DB
- google-api-python-client already a project dependency
- Test patterns: mock external deps via sys.modules patching, use catalog_db fixture, tmp_path for file I/O

The upload/__init__.py currently exists but is empty. It needs to export the public symbols from both new modules.

Review fixes (steps 21-26):
- thumbnail.py _extract_best_frame: cv2.VideoCapture handle leaked on exception — need try/finally around scoring loop so cap.release() is unconditional
- thumbnail.py _extract_best_frame: cv2.imwrite return value unchecked — silent failure returns path to non-existent file; must check and return None on False
- youtube.py upload_video: missing break after successful upload in outer retry for-loop — runs redundant no-op iterations and obscures intent

prerequisites.description: Update autopilot/upload/__init__.py to export public symbols from youtube and thumbnail modules (UploadError, upload_video, ThumbnailError, extract_best_thumbnail)

steps.description: Write failing tests for youtube.py public API surface: UploadError importable and is Exception, upload_video signature has (narrative_id, video_path, db, config) params, __all__ exports UploadError and upload_video

Create autopilot/upload/youtube.py with UploadError exception class, upload_video stub function with correct signature (narrative_id: str, video_path: Path, db: CatalogDB, config: YouTubeConfig) -> str, and __all__ exports. Function raises NotImplementedError for now.

Write failing tests for OAuth credential loading: test_loads_credentials_from_config_path (mocks google.oauth2.credentials.Credentials.from_authorized_user_file), test_raises_upload_error_when_credentials_file_missing (no creds file -> UploadError), test_refreshes_expired_credentials (mocks creds.expired=True + creds.refresh_token set -> calls creds.refresh with google.auth.transport.requests.Request)

Implement _load_credentials(credentials_path: Path) helper that loads OAuth2 credentials from file via Credentials.from_authorized_user_file, checks creds.expired and creds.refresh_token, refreshes via creds.refresh(Request()) if needed, and raises UploadError on missing file or refresh failure

Write failing tests for metadata assembly: test_builds_title_from_narrative (title from db.get_narrative), test_builds_description_from_script (description from db.get_narrative_script), test_tags_from_activity_labels_and_detections (extracts unique object class names from detections JSON + activity cluster labels), test_uses_config_privacy_status_and_category (privacyStatus and categoryId from YouTubeConfig)

Implement _build_upload_metadata(narrative_id, db, config) that queries CatalogDB for narrative title/description, narrative_script for full description, activity_clusters for labels (tags), and detections for unique object class names (tags). Returns dict with 'snippet' (title, description, tags, categoryId) and 'status' (privacyStatus) suitable for YouTube API body.

Write failing tests for the full upload_video flow: test_upload_calls_youtube_api_insert (mocks googleapiclient.discovery.build, verifies videos().insert() called with MediaFileUpload), test_upload_stores_result_in_db (catalog_db fixture: verifies db.insert_upload called with youtube_video_id and youtube_url after upload), test_upload_returns_youtube_url (returns 'https://youtu.be/<video_id>'), test_upload_raises_on_api_error (HttpError from API wrapped as UploadError)

Implement the full upload_video() function: load credentials via _load_credentials, build metadata via _build_upload_metadata, create YouTube service via googleapiclient.discovery.build('youtube', 'v3', credentials=creds), call videos().insert() with MediaFileUpload(video_path, resumable=True), execute resumable upload via next_chunk() loop, store result in DB via db.insert_upload, return YouTube URL

Write failing tests for upload_video edge cases: test_narrative_not_found_raises_upload_error (narrative_id not in DB -> UploadError), test_video_file_not_found_raises_upload_error (video_path doesn't exist -> UploadError), test_resumable_upload_retry (simulates HttpError on first next_chunk, success on retry)

Add input validation and retry logic to upload_video: validate narrative exists in DB (raise UploadError if not), validate video_path.exists() (raise UploadError if not), implement resumable upload retry loop with exponential backoff for transient HttpError (max 3 retries)

Write failing tests for thumbnail.py public API surface: ThumbnailError importable and is Exception, extract_best_thumbnail signature has (narrative_id, video_path, db) params, __all__ exports ThumbnailError and extract_best_thumbnail

Create autopilot/upload/thumbnail.py with ThumbnailError exception class, extract_best_thumbnail stub with correct signature (narrative_id: str, video_path: Path, db: CatalogDB) -> Path, and __all__ exports. Function raises NotImplementedError for now.

Write failing tests for frame scoring functions: test_sharpness_score_uses_laplacian_variance (mock cv2.Laplacian, verify variance computed on result), test_rule_of_thirds_score_high_at_intersections (numpy frame with subject bbox at thirds intersection scores higher than bbox at dead center), test_detection_confidence_score_aggregates (max confidence from list of detections dicts), test_combined_score_weighted (verify the 3 component scores are combined with weights summing to 1.0)

Implement scoring functions: _sharpness_score(frame: ndarray) -> float using cv2.Laplacian variance, _rule_of_thirds_score(frame_shape, detections: list[dict]) -> float using bbox center distances to 4 thirds-grid intersection points, _detection_confidence_score(detections: list[dict]) -> float using max confidence across all detections, _combined_score(sharpness, thirds, confidence) -> float with configurable weights (default: 0.3 sharpness, 0.3 thirds, 0.4 confidence)

Write failing tests for frame extraction: test_extracts_best_frame_as_jpeg (mocks cv2.VideoCapture to return frames, verifies highest-scoring frame is saved as JPEG via cv2.imwrite), test_samples_frames_at_intervals (verifies frames are sampled every N frames rather than reading every frame by checking cap.set calls), test_returns_thumbnail_path (returns Path to the saved JPEG in video_path.parent)

Implement _extract_candidate_frames() that opens video with cv2.VideoCapture, gets total frame count and fps, samples frames at ~1fps intervals using cap.set(CAP_PROP_POS_FRAMES), scores each with combined scoring, and saves the best frame as JPEG via cv2.imwrite. Wire into extract_best_thumbnail flow.

Write failing tests for thumbnail upload to YouTube: test_uploads_thumbnail_via_api (mocks YouTube service thumbnails().set() API call with youtube_video_id from uploads table), test_thumbnail_upload_error_logged_not_raised (API error logged as warning, does not raise), test_no_youtube_upload_skips_thumbnail_upload (when no upload record in DB, still returns thumbnail path without calling thumbnails API)

Implement YouTube thumbnail upload in extract_best_thumbnail: check uploads table via db.get_upload(narrative_id) for youtube_video_id, if present load credentials and call thumbnails().set(videoId=youtube_video_id, media_body=MediaFileUpload(thumb_path, mimetype='image/jpeg')), handle API errors gracefully (log warning, don't raise). Return thumbnail path unconditionally.

Write failing test for scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py: test_oauth_flow_saves_credentials (mocks google_auth_oauthlib.flow.InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file and run_local_server, verifies credentials serialized and written to output path), test_main_requires_client_secrets_arg (verifies argparse requires --client-secrets)

Create scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py: argparse with --client-secrets (required) and --output (default ~/.config/autopilot/youtube_oauth.json). Uses InstalledAppFlow.from_client_secrets_file with YOUTUBE_UPLOAD_SCOPE, calls flow.run_local_server(port=0), serializes credentials to JSON (creds.to_json()), writes to output path. Includes if __name__ == '__main__' block.

Write failing test for VideoCapture resource cleanup: test_video_capture_released_on_scoring_exception in TestFrameExtraction. Mock cv2.VideoCapture to return a mock cap object, and mock cv2.Laplacian to raise RuntimeError('malformed frame') on the first call. Call _extract_best_frame(video_path, detections) and expect the RuntimeError to propagate. Assert that mock_cap.release() was called exactly once despite the exception, verifying the try/finally guard around the VideoCapture loop.

Fix resource leak in thumbnail.py _extract_best_frame() (lines 145-168): wrap the frame sampling for-loop (lines 153-166) and the post-loop best_frame check in a try block, move cap.release() from line 168 into a finally clause. Structure: cap = cv2.VideoCapture(...); try: <get props, loop, find best> finally: cap.release(). This ensures the OS file handle is released regardless of exceptions from cv2.Laplacian, numpy ops, or array operations on malformed frames.

Write failing test for cv2.imwrite failure handling: test_imwrite_failure_returns_none in TestFrameExtraction. Mock cv2.VideoCapture to return valid frames, mock cv2.Laplacian to return a valid array, but mock cv2.imwrite to return False (simulating disk full / permission denied). Call _extract_best_frame(video_path, []) and assert it returns None instead of a Path, verifying the imwrite return value is checked.

Fix silent failure in thumbnail.py _extract_best_frame() (lines 173-175): replace unconditional 'cv2.imwrite(str(thumb_path), best_frame); return thumb_path' with 'ok = cv2.imwrite(str(thumb_path), best_frame); if not ok: logger.warning("Failed to write thumbnail: %s", thumb_path); return None; return thumb_path'. The caller extract_best_thumbnail already handles None by raising ThumbnailError.

Write failing test for upload retry loop break-on-success: test_upload_breaks_after_first_success in TestUploadVideoFlow. Mock next_chunk() to return (None, {'id': 'ok123'}) on the first call. After upload_video returns, assert next_chunk was called exactly once (call_count == 1). If the outer for-loop doesn't break, next_chunk would be called additional times (as no-ops) on subsequent iterations. This verifies the break statement exists.

Fix retry logic in youtube.py upload_video() (lines 192-210): add 'break' immediately after the 'while response is None' block (after line 197) exits without raising an exception. This ensures the outer 'for attempt in range(max_retries)' loop terminates after the first successful upload instead of running redundant no-op iterations on attempts 1 and 2.

design_decisions.decision: Use resumable uploads via MediaFileUpload(resumable=True) with next_chunk() loop
design_decisions.rationale: Video files can be large (multi-GB). Resumable uploads handle network interruptions gracefully and are the recommended approach for the YouTube Data API. Each upload costs 1600 quota units out of 10K/day (6 uploads max), so retry on failure is critical to avoid wasting quota.

Credential handling (load from file, check expiry, refresh token) is complex enough to warrant its own function for testability. Both upload_video and thumbnail upload (via extract_best_thumbnail) need credentials, so this avoids duplication.

design_decisions.decision: Frame scoring uses weighted combination of 3 metrics: YOLO detection confidence (0.4), rule-of-thirds composition (0.3), Laplacian sharpness (0.3)
design_decisions.rationale: These are the three criteria specified in the task. Separating them into individual scoring functions allows independent testing and future tuning. Detection confidence gets highest weight since frames with clear subjects make the best thumbnails.

design_decisions.decision: Sample frames at ~1fps intervals rather than scoring every frame
design_decisions.rationale: Scoring every frame of a multi-minute video would be slow and unnecessary for thumbnail selection. Sampling at ~1fps provides sufficient coverage while keeping computation manageable. Uses cap.set(CAP_PROP_POS_FRAMES) for efficient seeking.

design_decisions.decision: Use cv2 (OpenCV) for frame extraction and Laplacian sharpness, numpy for array operations
design_decisions.rationale: OpenCV is the standard for video frame access and image processing. numpy is already a project dependency. cv2 provides VideoCapture for frame extraction and Laplacian filter for sharpness measurement. Pillow (already a dependency) is available but cv2 is more appropriate for video frame operations.

design_decisions.decision: extract_best_thumbnail returns the JPEG path even if YouTube thumbnail upload fails or is skipped
design_decisions.rationale: The thumbnail extraction is useful independently of whether it gets uploaded to YouTube. If no YouTube upload exists yet (e.g., upload_video hasn't been called), the thumbnail is still generated for potential later use. Thumbnail upload failure is logged as warning, not raised.

design_decisions.decision: Mock external Google API dependencies via unittest.mock patching, consistent with existing test patterns
design_decisions.rationale: The project consistently mocks heavy external deps (see test_voiceover.py patching kokoro/soundfile, test_broll.py patching requests). This avoids needing google-api-python-client and google-auth installed in the test environment and avoids hitting real APIs.

design_decisions.decision: Tags sourced from activity cluster labels AND unique YOLO object class names from detections
design_decisions.rationale: Task specifies tags from 'activity labels + detected object classes'. Activity clusters have label fields. Detections JSON contains class_name strings. Combined and deduplicated, these provide semantic YouTube tags for discoverability.

design_decisions.decision: VideoCapture handle guarded by try/finally for unconditional release
design_decisions.rationale: Review identified that cap.release() was only on the happy path. In a batch pipeline processing many videos, leaked file descriptors from exceptions in scoring functions (cv2.Laplacian, numpy ops) would exhaust OS limits. try/finally is the standard Python pattern for resource cleanup without context-manager support.

design_decisions.decision: cv2.imwrite return value checked, returning None on failure
design_decisions.rationale: cv2.imwrite silently returns False on failure (disk full, permission denied, bad path) without raising. Returning the path unconditionally violates the function contract — callers would receive a path to a non-existent file. Returning None lets the existing error handling in extract_best_thumbnail raise ThumbnailError.

design_decisions.decision: Break out of retry loop after first successful upload
design_decisions.rationale: Without the break, after a successful upload the outer for-loop runs remaining iterations as no-ops (inner while skips since response is not None). While not causing double uploads today, it wastes time and makes the retry logic fragile for future modifications.

reuse.how: Use existing CRUD methods to store/retrieve YouTube upload records (narrative_id, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status) without any schema changes
Use existing config with privacy_status, default_category, credentials_path fields. No config changes needed.
Retrieve narrative title/description for YouTube video title/description, and script JSON for extended description
Retrieve activity cluster labels to use as YouTube video tags
Retrieve YOLO detection JSON for: (1) object class names as YouTube tags, (2) detection confidences for thumbnail frame scoring
Use for testing upload_video and extract_best_thumbnail DB interactions with autocommit
Already listed in project dependencies. Provides googleapiclient.discovery.build, googleapiclient.http.MediaFileUpload for YouTube API interaction.

reuse.where: All existing modules (voiceover.py, objects.py, broll.py, etc.)
reuse.how: Follow established pattern: custom XxxError class, __all__ list, TYPE_CHECKING guard for type-only imports of CatalogDB/config types, logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
